title: Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup

seo.title: Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup: Authentic Indian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Warm up with our authentic Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup recipe! This Indian spiced soup is packed with flavor, healthy lentils, and aromatic spices. Get the easy step-by-step guide.

seo.imageAlt: A hearty bowl of authentic Indian Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup, garnished with fresh herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of our homemade Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup, bursting with Indian flavors.

description: Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup is a delightful and warming Indian creation, blending aromatic spices with hearty lentils for a truly comforting experience. This vibrant soup, with its rich history, offers a burst of flavors from turmeric, cumin, and ginger, making it a perfect meal for any season. Enjoy a bowl of this wholesome and flavorful soup, a true testament to Indian culinary artistry.

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Chop the onion, mince the garlic, grate the ginger, dice the tomatoes, carrot, and apple (if using). Gather all your spices.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat vegetable oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter. Then add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Toast Spices and Add Tomatoes
instructions.text: Stir in the curry powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly, until aromatic. Add the diced tomatoes (or crushed tomatoes) and cook for 2-3 minutes, letting them break down slightly.

instructions.title: Combine Lentils and Broth
instructions.text: Add the rinsed lentils, diced carrot, and diced apple (if using) to the pot. Pour in the vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.

instructions.title: Achieve Desired Consistency
instructions.text: Remove the cinnamon stick and bay leaf. For a smoother soup, use an immersion blender to partially blend the soup, leaving some texture. Alternatively, scoop out about 1-2 cups of the soup, blend it in a regular blender, and return it to the pot. Stir in the full-fat coconut milk.

instructions.title: Season and Finish
instructions.text: Season the soup with salt and black pepper to taste. Stir in the fresh lemon juice. Heat through for another 2-3 minutes without boiling, allowing the flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup into bowls. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve immediately with a side of warm naan bread or rice for a complete and satisfying Indian meal.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, temper a small amount of ghee or oil with curry leaves and a pinch of asafoetida, then pour over the finished soup just before serving.
If you prefer a sweeter soup, increase the amount of apple or add a touch of brown sugar. For more heat, add a finely chopped green chili with the garlic and ginger.

Leftover Mulligatawny soup tastes even better the next day as the flavors deepen. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop or in the microwave.
article: Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup is a beloved Indian Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
